web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://cursor.com/en-US/docs#main-content) ## Command Palette Search for a command to run... ## Get Started [Welcome](https://cursor.com/docs) [Quickstart](https://cursor.com/docs/get-started/quickstart) Models & Pricing [Changelog](https://cursor.com/changelog) ## Agent [Overview](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/overview) [Agents Window](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/agents-window) [Planning](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/plan-mode) [Prompting](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/prompting) [Debugging](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/debug-mode) Tools [Security](https://cursor.com/docs/agent/security) ## Customizing [Plugins](https://cursor.com/docs/plugins) [Rules](https://cursor.com/docs/rules) [Skills](https://cursor.com/docs/skills) [Subagents](https://cursor.com/docs/subagents) [Hooks](https://cursor.com/docs/hooks) [MCP](https://cursor.com/docs/mcp) ## Cloud Agents [Overview](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent) [Setup](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/setup) [Capabilities](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/capabilities) [Self-Hosted](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/self-hosted) [Bugbot](https://cursor.com/docs/bugbot) [Automations](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/automations) [Best Practices](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/best-practices) [Security & Network](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/security-network) [Settings](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/settings) [API](https://cursor.com/docs/cloud-agent/api/endpoints) ## Integrations [Slack](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/slack) [Linear](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/linear) [GitHub](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/github) [GitLab](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/gitlab) [JetBrains](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/jetbrains) [Xcode](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/xcode) [Deeplinks](https://cursor.com/docs/reference/deeplinks) ## CLI [Overview](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/overview) [Installation](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/installation) [Capabilities](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/using) [Shell Mode](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/shell-mode) [ACP](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/acp) [Headless / CI](https://cursor.com/docs/cli/headless) Reference ## Teams & Enterprise Teams Enterprise Get Started # Cursor Documentation Cursor is an AI editor and coding agent. Use it to understand your codebase, plan and build features, fix bugs, review changes, and work with the tools you already use. ![Welcome to Cursor, the AI editor and coding agent](https://cursor.com/docs-static/_next/image?url=%2Fdocs-static%2Fimages%2Fagent%2Freview.jpg&w=1920&q=75&dpl=dpl_9zWDz3izVdg27LUFQbNzaLYcu8Pt) ## [Start here](https://cursor.com/en-US/docs\#start-here) [Get started\\ \\ Go from install to your first useful change in Cursor](https://cursor.com/docs/get-started/quickstart) [Models & Pricing\\ \\ Compare models, usage pools, and plan pricing](https://cursor.com/docs/models-and-pricing) [Changelog\\ \\ Stay up to date with the latest features and improvements](https://www.cursor.com/changelog) ## [What you can do with Cursor](https://cursor.com/en-US/docs\#what-you-can-do-with-cursor) [Understand your code\\ \\ Trace how a repo fits together and find the right places to start](https://cursor.com/learn/understanding-your-codebase) [Plan and build features\\ \\ Scope changes, use Plan Mode, and ship bigger work with confidence](https://cursor.com/learn/creating-features) [Find and fix bugs\\ \\ Reproduce issues, narrow the root cause, and verify the fix](https://cursor.com/learn/finding-fixing-bugs) [Review changes\\ \\ Inspect diffs, run checks, and catch problems before you merge](https://cursor.com/learn/reviewing-testing) [Customize Cursor\\ \\ Use rules, skills, and prompts that match how your team works](https://cursor.com/docs/rules) [Connect your workflow\\ \\ Work with GitHub, GitLab, JetBrains, Slack, Linear, and more](https://cursor.com/docs/integrations/github) ## [Models](https://cursor.com/en-US/docs\#models) See all model attributes on the [Models & Pricing](https://cursor.com/docs/models-and-pricing) page. | Name | Default Context | Max Mode | Capabilities | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ![Anthropic](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/anthropic-dark.svg)![Anthropic](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/anthropic-light.svg)
[Claude 4.6 Opus](https://cursor.com/docs/models/claude-opus-4-6) | 200k | 1M | AgentThinking | | ![Anthropic](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/anthropic-dark.svg)![Anthropic](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/anthropic-light.svg)
[Claude 4.6 Sonnet](https://cursor.com/docs/models/claude-4-6-sonnet) | 200k | 1M | AgentThinking | | ![Cursor](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/cursor.svg)
[Composer 2](https://cursor.com/docs/models/cursor-composer-2) | 200k | - | AgentThinking | | ![Google](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/google.svg)
[Gemini 3.1 Pro](https://cursor.com/docs/models/gemini-3-1-pro) | 200k | 1M | AgentThinking | | ![OpenAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/openai-dark.svg)![OpenAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/openai-light.svg)
[GPT-5.3 Codex](https://cursor.com/docs/models/gpt-5-3-codex) | 272k | - | AgentThinking | | ![OpenAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/openai-dark.svg)![OpenAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/openai-light.svg)
[GPT-5.4](https://cursor.com/docs/models/gpt-5-4) | 272k | 1M | AgentThinking | | ![xAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/xai-dark.svg)![xAI](https://cursor.com/docs-static/images/providers/xai-light.svg)
[Grok 4.20](https://cursor.com/docs/models/grok-4-20) | 200k | 2M | AgentThinking | Show more models ## [More resources](https://cursor.com/en-US/docs\#more-resources) [Downloads\\ \\ Get Cursor for your computer](https://cursor.com/downloads) [Help\\ \\ Find answers to common questions and troubleshooting guides](https://cursor.com/help) Support For account and billing questions, contact our support team English - English - 简体中文 - 日本語 - 繁體中文 - Español - Français - Português - 한국어 - Русский - Türkçe - Bahasa Indonesia - Deutsch Agent Tokenizer OffContext: 0/200k (0%) Open chat

web.title: OpenRouter
web.description: One API for Any Model. Access all major models through a single, unified interface. OpenAI SDK works out of the box.
web.markdown:
# The Unified Interface For LLMs

Better [prices](https://openrouter.ai/models?order=pricing-low-to-high), better [uptime](https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/uptime-optimization), no subscriptions.

[Get API Key](https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys) [Explore Models\\

\\
**One API for Any Model** \\
\\
Access all major models through a single, unified interface. OpenAI SDK works out of the box.\\
\\
Browse all](https://openrouter.ai/models) [anthropic/claude-opus-4.6\\
\\
Model routing visualization\\
\\
![Favicon for Google](https://openrouter.ai/images/icons/GoogleVertex.svg)\\
\\
![Favicon for Anthropic](https://openrouter.ai/images/icons/Anthropic.svg)\\
\\
![Favicon for Amazon Bedrock](https://openrouter.ai/images/icons/Bedrock.svg)\\
\\
**Higher Availability** \\
\\
Reliable AI models via our distributed infrastructure. Fall back to other providers when one goes down.\\
\\
Learn more](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/uptime-optimization) [![Performance graph](https://openrouter.ai/_next/image?url=%2Fperf-light.png&w=640&q=75)![Performance graph](https://openrouter.ai/_next/image?url=%2Fperf-dark.png&w=640&q=75)\\
\\
**Price and Performance** \\
\\
Keep costs in check without sacrificing speed. OpenRouter runs at the edge for minimal latency between your users and their inference.\\
\\
Learn more](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/provider-selection#provider-sorting) [Data policy visualization\\
\\
**Custom Data Policies** \\
\\
Protect your organization with fine grained data policies. Ensure prompts only go to the models and providers you trust.\\
\\
View docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/privacy/logging)

[**Featured Models**](https://openrouter.ai/models?order=top-weekly)

300+ active models on 60+providers

### Signup

Create an account to get started. You can set up an org for your team later.

Google

GitHub

MetaMask

2

### Buy credits

Credits can be used with any model or provider.

Apr 1

$99

Mar 30

$10

3

### Get your API key

Create an API key and start making requests. [Fully OpenAI compatible](https://openrouter.ai/docs/quickstart).

OPENROUTER\_API\_KEY

••••••••••••••••

[**Explore Models** \\

Discover AI models across our collection, from all major labs and providers.\\

View models](https://openrouter.ai/models) [**Model & App Rankings** \\

[**Auto Exacto: Adaptive Quality Routing, On by Default** \\
\\
OpenRouter now helps you use the tool-calling providers for new models automatically\\
\\
3/12/2026](https://openrouter.ai/announcements/auto-exacto) [**February Release Spotlight** \\
\\
Benchmarks on model pages, a new free model router, and improved cost transparency between providers.\\
\\
2/23/2026](https://openrouter.ai/announcements/february-release-spotlight) [**OpenRouter Outages on February 17 and 19, 2026** \\
\\
What happened and what have we done to prevent this happening again?\\
\\
2/20/2026](https://openrouter.ai/announcements/openrouter-outages-on-february-17-and-19-2026)

# [Cursor is practically unusable with any new model through OpenRouter](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979)

[Support](https://forum.cursor.com/c/support/21) [Help](https://forum.cursor.com/c/support/help/8)

You have selected **0** posts.

[select all](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979)

[cancel selecting](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979)

[Nov 2025](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979/1 "Jump to the first post")

2 / 4


Nov 2025


[Dec 2025](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979/4)

## post by Ralek on Nov 24, 2025

[![](https://avatars.discourse-cdn.com/v4/letter/r/bc79bd/48.png)](https://forum.cursor.com/u/ralek)

[Ralek](https://forum.cursor.com/u/ralek)

[Nov 2025](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979 "Post date")

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-where-does-the-bug-appear-featureproduct-1) Where does the bug appear (feature/product)?

Cursor IDE

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-describe-the-bug-2) Describe the Bug

I use OpenRouter to access models. But when I try, I get errors like these:

GPT5.1:

```swift
Request failed with status code 400: {"error":{"message":"Input required: specify \"prompt\" or \"messages\"","code":400},"user_id":"user_2smDyoir6cVtTliFhxZDiolmcWC"}
```

Gemini 3:

Will list the current directory or do some other tool call, then get this error:

```swift
Request failed with status code 400: {"error":{"message":"Provider returned error","code":400,"metadata":{"raw":"Gemini models require OpenRouter reasoning details to be preserved in each request. Please refer to our docs: https://openrouter.ai/docs/use-cases/reasoning-tokens#preserving-reasoning-blocks. Upstream error: {\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": 400,\n    \"message\": \"Unable to submit request because function call `default_api:list_dir` in the 2. content block is missing a `thought_signature`. Learn more: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/thought-signatures\",\n    \"status\": \"INVALID_ARGUMENT\"\n  }\n}\n","provider_name":"Google"}},"user_id":"user_2smDyoir6cVtTliFhxZDiolmcWC"}
```

Claude sonnet 4.5:

```css
Request failed with status code 500: {"error":{"message":"Internal Server Error","code":500}}
```

Claude sonnet 4:

```css
Request failed with status code 500: {"error":{"message":"Internal Server Error","code":500}}
```

Only last gen models work at all.

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-steps-to-reproduce-3) Steps to Reproduce

Use any modern model through OpenRouter

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-operating-system-4) Operating System

Linux

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-current-cursor-version-menu-about-cursor-copy-5) Current Cursor Version (Menu → About Cursor → Copy)

Version: 2.1.26

VSCode Version: 1.105.1

Commit: f628a4761be40b8869ca61a6189cafd14756dff0

Date: 2025-11-24T05:39:06.655Z

Electron: 37.7.0

Chromium: 138.0.7204.251

Node.js: 22.20.0

V8: 13.8.258.32-electron.0

OS: Linux x64 6.17.0-6-generic

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-for-ai-issues-which-model-did-you-use-6) For AI issues: which model did you use?

Claude sonnet 4, Claude saonet 4.5, Gemini 3, GPT 5.1

### [Heading link](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979\#p-297807-for-ai-issues-add-request-id-with-privacy-disabled-7) For AI issues: add Request ID with privacy disabled

Request ID: def4ae9a-9a68-430c-8a8d-667afe8bd7ef

{“error”:“ERROR\_OPENAI”,“details”:{“title”:“Unable to reach the model provider”,“detail”:“We encountered an issue when using your API key: Provider was unable to process your request\\n\\nAPI Error:\\n\\n`\nRequest failed with status code 500: {\"error\":{\"message\":\"Internal Server Error\",\"code\":500}}\n`”,“additionalInfo”:{},“buttons”:,“planChoices”:},“isExpected”:true}

ConnectError: \[invalid\_argument\] Error

at kZc.$endAiConnectTransportReportError (vscode-file://vscode-app/usr/share/cursor/resources/app/out/vs/workbench/workbench.desktop.main.js:6330:408452)

[![](https://avatars.discourse-cdn.com/v4/letter/k/49beb7/48.png)](https://forum.cursor.com/u/Koleboxx "Koleboxx")

[![](https://sea3.discourse-cdn.com/cursor1/user_avatar/forum.cursor.com/deanrie/48/1263_2.png)](https://forum.cursor.com/u/deanrie "deanrie")

## post by deanrie on Nov 25, 2025

[![](https://sea3.discourse-cdn.com/cursor1/user_avatar/forum.cursor.com/deanrie/48/1263_2.png)](https://forum.cursor.com/u/deanrie)

[Dean Rie](https://forum.cursor.com/u/deanrie)

[Nov 2025](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979/2 "Post date")

Hey, thanks for the report. OpenRouter is not officially supported by Cursor, which is why you’re running into compatibility issues with newer models. The errors you’re seeing (missing thought\_signature, request format problems) happen because Cursor’s API implementation is built for officially supported providers.

Officially supported options:

1. Use a Cursor Pro subscription (includes Claude, GPT-5, and other models)
2. Add your own API keys for official providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) in Settings → Models

Some older OpenRouter models might still work by chance, but there’s no guarantee they’ll stay compatible, especially as model APIs change over time (reasoning tokens, new function calling formats, etc).

If you need specific models that aren’t available through the official providers in Cursor, it’s better to use direct API keys from those providers instead of routing through OpenRouter.

## post by Koleboxx on Nov 26, 2025

[![](https://avatars.discourse-cdn.com/v4/letter/k/49beb7/48.png)](https://forum.cursor.com/u/koleboxx)

[Koleboxx](https://forum.cursor.com/u/koleboxx)

[Nov 2025](https://forum.cursor.com/t/cursor-is-practically-unusable-with-any-new-model-through-openrouter/143979/3 "Post date")

Since there is Bedrock and Azure integration, when will we see Vertex AI? OpenRouter was way to go, now I have to run custom local proxy exposing openAI endpoint to curosor and calling Google SDKs, which is not ideal as I run into occasional issues and need to change the implementation.

22 days later

# [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#cursor-+-openrouter-setup)  Cursor + OpenRouter Setup

This guide walks you through setting up Claude-Mem in Cursor using OpenRouter. OpenRouter provides access to 100+ AI models from various providers, including several free options.

**Model variety:** Access Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, Llama, Mistral, and many more through a single API key.

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#step-1-get-an-openrouter-api-key)  Step 1: Get an OpenRouter API Key

1. Go to [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/)
2. Sign up or sign in
3. Navigate to [API Keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
4. Click **Create Key**
5. Copy your API key - you’ll need it in Step 3

**Free models available:** OpenRouter offers free versions of several models including Gemini Flash and others. Check the [model list](https://openrouter.ai/models?show_free=true) for current free options.

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#step-2-clone-and-build-claude-mem)  Step 2: Clone and Build Claude-Mem

```
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem.git
cd claude-mem

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Build the project
bun run build
```

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#step-3-configure-openrouter-provider)  Step 3: Configure OpenRouter Provider

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#option-a-interactive-setup-recommended)  Option A: Interactive Setup (Recommended)

Run the setup wizard which guides you through everything:

```
bun run cursor:setup
```

When prompted for provider, select **OpenRouter**.

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#option-b-manual-configuration)  Option B: Manual Configuration

Create the settings file manually:

```
# Create settings directory
mkdir -p ~/.claude-mem

# Create settings file with OpenRouter configuration
cat > ~/.claude-mem/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "CLAUDE_MEM_PROVIDER": "openrouter",
  "CLAUDE_MEM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY": "YOUR_OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
}
EOF
```

Replace `YOUR_OPENROUTER_API_KEY` with your actual API key.Then install hooks and start the worker:

```
bun run cursor:install
bun run worker:start
```

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#step-4-restart-cursor)  Step 4: Restart Cursor

Close and reopen Cursor IDE for the hooks to take effect.

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#step-5-verify-installation)  Step 5: Verify Installation

```
# Check worker is running
bun run worker:status

# Check hooks are installed
bun run cursor:status
```

Open [http://localhost:37777](http://localhost:37777/) to see the memory viewer.

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#recommended-models)  Recommended Models

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#free-models)  Free Models

| Model | Provider | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `google/gemini-2.0-flash-exp:free` | Google | Fast, capable |
| `xiaomi/mimo-v2-flash:free` | Xiaomi | Good general purpose |

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#paid-models-low-cost)  Paid Models (Low Cost)

| Model | Approx. Cost | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `anthropic/claude-3-haiku` | ~$0.25/1M tokens | Fast, efficient |
| `google/gemini-flash-1.5` | ~$0.075/1M tokens | Great value |
| `mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct` | ~$0.07/1M tokens | Budget option |

To specify a model, add to your settings:

```
{
  "CLAUDE_MEM_PROVIDER": "openrouter",
  "CLAUDE_MEM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY": "your-key",
  "CLAUDE_MEM_OPENROUTER_MODEL": "google/gemini-2.0-flash-exp:free"
}
```

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#cost-management)  Cost Management

OpenRouter charges per token. To manage costs:

1. **Use free models:** Several high-quality free models are available
2. **Monitor usage:** Check your [OpenRouter dashboard](https://openrouter.ai/activity)
3. **Set spending limits:** Configure limits in OpenRouter settings

**Cost awareness:** Unlike Gemini’s free tier, OpenRouter paid models charge per request. Monitor your usage if using paid models.

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#troubleshooting)  Troubleshooting

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#%E2%80%9Dopenrouter-api-key-not-configured%E2%80%9D)  ”OpenRouter API key not configured”

Ensure your settings file exists with the correct format:

```
cat ~/.claude-mem/settings.json
```

Should output something like:

```
{
  "CLAUDE_MEM_PROVIDER": "openrouter",
  "CLAUDE_MEM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY": "sk-or-..."
}
```

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#model-not-found)  Model not found

1. Check the model ID is correct at [OpenRouter Models](https://openrouter.ai/models)
2. Some models may require payment - check if you have credits
3. Free models have `:free` suffix in their ID

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#rate-limits)  Rate limits

OpenRouter rate limits vary by model and your account tier. If you hit limits:

1. Wait briefly and retry
2. Consider upgrading your OpenRouter account tier
3. Switch to a less popular model

### [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#api-errors)  API errors

Check the worker logs for details:

```
bun run worker:logs
```

Common issues:

- Invalid API key (regenerate at OpenRouter)
- Insufficient credits for paid models
- Model temporarily unavailable

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#switching-providers-later)  Switching Providers Later

You can switch between OpenRouter, Gemini, and Claude SDK at any time by updating your settings. No restart required - changes take effect on the next observation.

```
{
  "CLAUDE_MEM_PROVIDER": "gemini"
}
```

## [​](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/openrouter-setup\#next-steps)  Next Steps

- [Cursor Integration Overview](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/index) \- All Cursor features
- [Gemini Setup](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/gemini-setup) \- Alternative free provider
- [Configuration Reference](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/configuration) \- All settings options

[Cursor + Gemini Setup](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/cursor/gemini-setup) [Gemini CLI Setup](https://docs.claude-mem.ai/gemini-cli/setup)

Ctrl+I

web.title: How to Use OpenRouter Models in Cursor - YouTube
web.description: I show you how to get OpenRouter configured within Cursor so you can use any OpenRouter model Links within the video: Cursor: ...
web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/n6j4nSt7qa0/maxresdefault.webp)
# [How to Use OpenRouter Models in Cursor](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n6j4nSt7qa0)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [hUndefined](http://www.youtube.com/@hUndefined)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-05-08T17:47:19-07:00
**Published at**: 2025-05-08T17:47:19-07:00
**Length**: 04:10
**Views**: 28140
**Likes**: 429
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
I show you how to get OpenRouter configured within Cursor so you can use any OpenRouter model

Links within the video:
Cursor: https://www.cursor.com/
OpenRouter: https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys
Override URL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1

#cursor #openrouter #ai
```

## Transcript

Let me show you how you can use open router models inside of cursor. Don't worry, it's really simple. The first thing that we need to do is create an account and sign in to open router.ai. Once you created an account, go to the upper right and hover over your icon. Then select keys from the drop-own menu. Now, it should take you to a page that looks something like this where we have our API keys. If you haven't created an API key before, you won't have anything here. And all we have to do to create one is click this button to create key. Then inside the popup, we just need to give it a name. So for my instance, I'm going to name it cursor. Once you give it a name, hit the create button. Now that your key is created, make sure that you hit this clipboard button immediately so that you can copy this key because once you close out of this modal, once you close out of this little window, this key is gone forever. I mean, you can't get it back. So this is the only chance that you have to copy it and put it into the place that we need to put it. If you happen to misclick or you accidentally lost this key, don't worry. You can always create a new one and use it in the process that I'm going to show you. So once we've hit the clipboard, we can exit out of this. Now that we're inside a cursor, we need to use that API key. So we need to go up to the upper right and click on this cog to open the cursor settings. Then once you're in settings, go over to the left to where it says models and click on it. Now you might have a bunch of models here that are selected. You'll want to deselect every single one that you have in this list. Once you've deselected all of them, go ahead and scroll down to the open API key section. Inside of here, this is where we're going to paste the API key that we have copied to our clipboard. Once you've pasted that key, you also have this little drop down down here that we need to click on. This is where we're going to override the OpenAI base URL with the one from Open Router. Now, I'm going to paste the Open Router URL here, but I will also have it in the description so you can go and get it there. Basically, it's just https/openouter.ai/api/v1. Once you add that URL, go ahead and hit the save button right next to it. Now that we have it saved, we need to add a model from Open Router onto our models page. To do that, we want to go back to open router.ai and then click on the models button up here. Now, depending on what models you want to use, you can search through this entire list. I personally always scroll down on this lefth hand sidebar and go down to the supported parameters and select tools. This will now only show models that allow for tools. And being able to interact with tools is really important if we want to use the agent inside of cursor. I'm also going to go to the prompt pricing and slide this slider all the way down to free and just show the free models that are tools. Now, this Mistral Small 3.124B model seems perfect for what we want to use. So, when we click on it, we come to this page and you'll notice we have this little clipboard button right next to the name of the model. We want to click that so that we copy the name of the model and then we'll go back to cursor. Inside of cursor at the bottom of the list of models, we want to add a new model. From here, we just paste in that model name that we just copied. Now we can just click on add model and it will automatically be checkarked so that we can use it. But before we use it, we need to verify the OpenAI API key that we're using for Open Router. So to do that, we click on this verify button underneath this section. Now it's asking us if we're sure we want to enable our own OpenAI API key. And yes, we do want to enable it. We know what we're doing. Once that completes, you should have this switch and it should switch on to green. And now we're able to use the open router models. So now in the upper right, let's use the agent. And right now it's set to auto. Since we don't have any other models selected in this models list, it will just choose the single model that we have. But we can also manually choose it. If we click this down arrow on the auto and then select mistral small 3.124b, we can select that. And now we're using that model for our prompting. Now I'm going to go ahead and add context here and select the compression go file. And I'm going to ask it what's in this file. Now it automatically generates the response and we get it directly from open router. Now we don't just have to use this mist 3.1 small 24B whatever model. We can use any model inside of open router. We can even use ones that are paid for. So cool. It says in summary this file provides functionality for compressing and resizing images which is exactly what it does. So this is neat because you're not just limited to OpenAI models or anthropic models or Google models. You can use literally any model that is supplied by Open Router. So that is how you can configure and use open router models inside of cursor.


## Endscreen
